response.justification: Strong software engineer with 18+ years of experience and proven software craftsmanship, but lacks the specific technical requirements for this industrial automation role. C++ expertise is 10+ years stale (last significant use 2014–2015), there is zero industrial automation or embedded systems background, and no demonstrated security practices or SDL/IEC 62443 compliance experience. Recent work is web and cloud-focused, not systems-level or industrial-domain development.
strengths: 18+ years of software development experience with proven ability to master diverse technologies and architectural patterns across web, mobile, desktop, and backend systems
Strong software craftsmanship foundation demonstrated through TDD practices, mentoring, open-source contributions, and designing scalable microservices architectures
Proven track record of optimizing performance-critical systems, including low-latency applications at Dunnhumby and scaling direct-mail platforms at Optilyz
concerns: C++ expertise is severely outdated—last professional C++ work was 6 months at National Instruments in 2014–2015; recent 9+ years focused on web technologies
Zero industrial automation or embedded systems background; career is entirely web services, cloud platforms, and mobile application development
No demonstrated expertise in security practices, Secure Development Lifecycle, IEC 62443 standards, or industrial cybersecurity required for the role
